title: Pete Hegseth, the embodiment of Trump's war in Iran
subhed: The US secretary of defense, a former Fox News host, has ardently defended the operation against the Islamic Republic. He has disregarded rules of engagement and boasted about American supremacy.

title: Anoush Ganjipour, philosopher: 'The Iranian people remain the first and last losers of the war in the Middle East'
subhed: The Iranian-born research fellow at the CNRS recounts from Beirut the 'extraordinary experience' of witnessing events in his home country from afar and firmly condemns the Israeli-American attacks.

title: War in Iran: 'Sailors are not just stuck in the Strait of Hormuz, they are prisoners on their ships'
subhed: Nearly 20,000 sailors remain stranded in the Arabian-Persian Gulf as Tehran blocks the maritime corridor. With international tensions escalating, seafaring is becoming far more perilous, writes Julien Bouissou.

title: PSG treads carefully after ultras display banner supporting star player facing rape trial
subhed: PSG has distanced itself from the banner displayed at the club's Parc des Princes stadium on February 25, while reiterating the 'presumption of innocence' for Achraf Hakimi, ordered to stand trial for rape.

title: Culling of foxes, crows and ravens is 'inefficient, economically unjustifiable and ethically questionable'
subhed: Researchers from the National Museum of Natural History examined the effects of systematically hunting 'species likely to cause damage.' They found that the cost of destroying these animals exceeded the agricultural damage they cause, without reducing that damage.

The ghosts of the new Chilean president's family
subhed: José Antonio Kast, who will be sworn in on Wednesday, makes no secret of his admiration for Augusto Pinochet's regime. He is less forthcoming about his father's past as a former Wehrmacht soldier and his family's role during the atrocities committed in Paine during the 1973 coup.

title: One week before France's local elections, electorate leans right, poll shows
subhed: With environmental issues taking a back seat, security issues coming to the fore and a clear increase in support for the far-right Rassemblement National, the survey conducted by Ipsos BVA for Le Monde, Cevipof and the Jean Jaurès Foundation paints an unprecedented picture of the French political landscape.

title: In southern Beirut: 'Dahiyeh is destroyed, but that changes nothing for us. We support Hezbollah.'
subhed: On the outskirts of the deserted Shiite neighborhoods in the southern suburbs of the Lebanese capital, militants and a few remaining residents watch helplessly as Israeli strikes destroy entire areas, day after day.

title: Trump's war gets caught in the oil trap
